summary: Finance & Fitness AI Tracker app (Version 5) deployed to Internet Computer. Adding full PWA support for proper home screen installation and fixing the live step counter which is not working in Chrome.
architectural_notes: App is deployed on Internet Computer and used as a PWA; requires proper manifest and service worker for home screen launch
known_issues: Live step counter not working in Chrome
App missing proper PWA configuration causing home screen icon launch failures
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- App icon for PWA manifest — solid dark background with a stylized 'FF' monogram in green and teal gradient, clean modern sans-serif, multiple sizes implied (pwa-icon.dim_512x512.png)

existing_features.title: Add full PWA support to the app so it installs and launches correctly from the home screen on Android Chrome. This includes: creating a web app manifest (manifest.json) with correct name, icons, start_url, display mode set to 'standalone', theme_color, and background_color; registering a service worker that caches the app shell for offline/standalone launch; and linking the manifest in index.html. The app must open successfully when launched via a home screen icon added through Chrome's 'Add to Home Screen' flow.

Fix the live step counter (useStepTracker hook and StepTrackerWidget component) so it correctly detects and counts steps in Chrome on Android. The DeviceMotion API in Chrome requires a permissions prompt (DeviceMotionEvent.requestPermission on some devices) and the event listener must be properly registered. Ensure the step detection algorithm works with Chrome's DeviceMotion event data, that permission is requested correctly, and that the live count updates visibly in the StepTrackerWidget UI in real time while walking.

new_feature_requests.summary: Add full PWA support (manifest, service worker) so app installs and launches correctly from home screen
Fix live step counter not working in Chrome
